subject: Sociology
title: Social Stratification Basics
description: A 60-second lesson on Social Stratification Basics.

hook.text: Every society in history has ranked its people. Some get more, others get less. The reasons change; the pattern doesn't.

buildup.text: Social stratification is the system by which society ranks people in a hierarchy of wealth, power, and prestige.

discovery.text: It's structural, not individual. Your position depends heavily on where you started: family, race, and geography.

twist.text: Meritocracy promises that talent rises. Data shows parental income still predicts a child's earnings better than grades.

climax.text: Stratification is self-reinforcing. Wealth buys education, education buys jobs, jobs buy wealth for the next generation.

punchline.text: The ladder exists. The question is who built it and for whom.

quiz.question: What does social stratification describe?
options: How society ranks people by wealth, power, and prestige
